name: BFS Clone
summary: Breadth-first walk the original graph; the first time a node is seen, create its clone and remember the pairing, then copy every edge into the clone.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the original graph as circles and edges, with an empty clone-map and an empty BFS queue. loop: a cursor visits nodes breadth-first from node 1; the visited node lights up. update: the first time a node is seen, a clone entry appears in the map (original -> clone) and the node is queued; every edge is copied into the clone's adjacency. return: the clone-map holds one entry per node and the cloned adjacency list mirrors the original — same shape, all-new nodes.

lineExplanations.3: Count the nodes in the graph.
lineExplanations.4: Prepare the node list for the drawing.
lineExplanations.5: Walk through every node to draw it.
lineExplanations.6: Add a node labelled with its 1-based value.
lineExplanations.7: Prepare the edge list for the drawing.
lineExplanations.8: Scan each node's neighbours to build the edges.
lineExplanations.10: Look at each neighbour in turn.
lineExplanations.12: Add each undirected edge only once (smaller endpoint first).
lineExplanations.13: Record the edge between the two nodes.
lineExplanations.14: Prepare the cloned adjacency list, one slot per node.
lineExplanations.15: Give every clone an empty neighbour list.
lineExplanations.16: Add an empty neighbour list for a clone.
lineExplanations.17: Create the map from an original node to its clone.
lineExplanations.18: If the graph is empty, there is nothing to clone.
lineExplanations.20: Seed the BFS queue with node 1.
lineExplanations.21: head marks the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.22: Clone node 1 and record the pairing.
lineExplanations.23: Breadth-first: process the queue until it is empty.
lineExplanations.24: Take the node at the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.25: Advance the queue front past it.
lineExplanations.27: Copy each neighbour edge in turn.
lineExplanations.29: Copy the edge into the clone's neighbour list.
lineExplanations.30: Check whether this neighbour has been cloned already.
lineExplanations.31: If it hasn't, it is a newly discovered node.
lineExplanations.32: Create its clone and record the pairing.
lineExplanations.33: Add it to the queue to process later.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: defines the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def cloneGraph(self, adjList): receives the graph as an adjacency list.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(adjList) is the number of nodes.
syntaxExplanations.4: nodes = [] starts an empty list of node descriptors.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): loops over node indices 0..n-1.
syntaxExplanations.6: nodes.append({...}) adds a node with a 1-based id and label.
syntaxExplanations.7: edges = [] starts an empty list of edge descriptors.
syntaxExplanations.8: for i in range(n): loops over node indices.
syntaxExplanations.9: adjList[i] is the neighbour list of node i+1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for j in range(len(neighbors)): loops over neighbour positions.
syntaxExplanations.12: if i + 1 < nb: keeps each undirected edge only once.
syntaxExplanations.13: edges.append({...}) records the edge from one node to another.
syntaxExplanations.14: result = [] will hold the cloned adjacency list.
syntaxExplanations.15: for i in range(n): loops over node indices.
syntaxExplanations.16: result.append([]) adds an empty neighbour list for a clone.
syntaxExplanations.17: cloned = {} maps an original node to its clone.
syntaxExplanations.18: if n == 0: is true for an empty graph.
syntaxExplanations.19: return result returns the empty clone.
syntaxExplanations.21: head = 0 points at the front of the queue.
syntaxExplanations.23: while head < len(queue): runs until every queued node is processed.
syntaxExplanations.24: queue[head] is the node at the front.
syntaxExplanations.26: adjList[node - 1] is this node's neighbour list.
syntaxExplanations.27: for j in range(len(neighbors)): loops over neighbours.
syntaxExplanations.29: result[node - 1].append(nb) copies the edge into the clone.
syntaxExplanations.30: nb in cloned tests whether the neighbour already has a clone.
syntaxExplanations.31: if not already: is true only for a newly seen node.
syntaxExplanations.32: cloned[nb] = nb creates the neighbour's clone.
syntaxExplanations.34: return result hands back the cloned graph.
